channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Imran Khan supporters breach lockdown in Pakistani capital, at least 6 people killed in violence
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Supporters seeking the release of imprisoned Pakistani former Prime Minister Imran Khan on Tuesday broke through a ring of shipping containers locking down the capital, Islamabad, while at least six people have died in protest-related violence.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Auto industry's shift toward EVs is expected to go on despite Trump threat to kill tax credits
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — If President-elect Donald Trump makes good on his threat to kill federal tax credits for electric vehicle purchases, it’s likely that fewer buyers will choose EVs.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER AP Auto Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukraine says Russian attack sets a new record for the number of drones used
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched 188 drones against most regions of Ukraine in a nighttime blitz, the Ukrainian air force said Tuesday, describing it as a record number of drones deployed in a single attack.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Philippine president and vice president clash in a feud that's testing an Asian democracy
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A major political storm set off by a bitter feud between the Philippine president and the vice president is testing an Asian democracy that has long been troubled by rowdy politics, poor law enforcement and bareface impunity.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rebel group takes key Myanmar border town and rare earth mining hub in setback for military regime
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A powerful rebel group has seized a key trading town in northeastern Myanmar on the Chinese border, taking control of a lucrative rare earth mining hub in another setback for the military-led government, according to witnesses.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: EU top diplomat says Israel has 'no excuses' to refuse a ceasefire with Hezbollah in US-French deal
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — The European Union’s top diplomat said Tuesday there were “no excuses” for Israel to refuse to accept a ceasefire with the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, saying all its security concerns had been addressed in the U.S.-French-brokered deal.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about the Menendez brothers' resentencing plea
item.description: A judge has delayed until January his decision on whether to resentence Erik and Lyle Menendez for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion 35 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Proposals to repeal no-fault divorce cause concern even as efforts stall
item.description: Warnings have been popping up on social media urging women who might be considering divorce to “pull the trigger” while they still can ever since President-elect Donald Trump and Vice President-elect JD Vance won the election.
item.dc:creator: Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 24-year-old man dies while hanging Christmas lights at California home
item.description: A 24-year-old man died by electrocution while installing Christmas lights on the roof of a home in Southern California, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oregon woman missing for days in Mt. Hood National Forest
item.description: The Clackamas County Sheriff’s Office is searching for a missing 61-year-old woman whose truck was spotted near a hiking trail in the Mt. Hood National Forest on Nov. 23.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Judge in LA delays until January decision on resentencing Menendez brothers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A judge on Monday delayed until January his decision on whether to resentence Erik and Lyle Menendez for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion 35 years ago, squashing their family’s hope the brothers would be released and home for the holidays.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Portland among cities slated for million-dollar housing market in 10 years: Analysis
item.description: Portland is set to welcome a million-dollar housing market in the next decade, according to a recent analysis from Realtor.com.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, NewsNation

item.title: White Florida woman sentenced to 25 years in prison for shooting Black neighbor in lengthy dispute
item.description: A white Florida woman who fatally shot a Black neighbor through her front door during an ongoing dispute over the neighbor’s boisterous children was sentenced Monday to 25 years in prison for her manslaughter conviction. Susan Lorincz, 60, was convicted in August of killing Ajike “A.J.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Couple charged in theft ring suspected of stealing $1 million in Lululemon clothes
item.description: A Connecticut couple has been charged in Minnesota with being part of a shoplifting ring suspected of stealing around $1 million in goods across the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Workers at Charlotte airport, an American Airlines hub, go on strike during Thanksgiving travel week
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Workers who clean airplanes, remove trash and help with wheelchairs at Charlotte's airport, one of the nation's busiest, went on strike Monday during a busy week of Thanksgiving travel to demand higher wages.
item.dc:creator: ERIK VERDUZCO and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Menendez brothers' resentencing request hearing pushed back to January
item.description: The Menendez brothers have waiting nearly three decades, and now they'll have to wait a little longer to hear their fates.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Texas DPS launches first border mounted patrol unit
item.description: Texas has graduated its first mounted border unit in an attempt to further secure the southern border and thwart Mexican smugglers.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Photo said to show Amelia Earhart's plane is something else entirely, exploration company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Special counsel moves to dismiss election interference, documents cases against President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith asked a federal judge on Monday to dismiss the case accusing President-elect Donald Trump of plotting to overturn the 2020 election, citing longstanding Justice Department policy shielding presidents from prosecution while in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: First right whales of season gorge on critical food off Massachusetts, giving hope for a strong year
item.description: Scientists who study a critically endangered species of whale that lives off New England said encouraging early signs suggest the animals could have a strong season for feeding and breeding.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Father of Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles found dead near LAX
item.description: Ryan Kobayashi, father of a 31-year-old Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles nearly two weeks ago, was found dead near Los Angeles International Airport early Sunday morning, officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: Drone team helps missing hunter safely escape woods
item.description: PESHTIGO, Wis. (WFRV) - Authorities in Marinette County, Wisconsin, say a missing hunter safely escaped from the woods thanks to help from the sheriff's office's drone team. According to a news release from the Marinette County Sheriff's Office, deputies responded at 5:03 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Nick Witwer, NewsNation

item.title: Blue state gun crackdown called out as threat to law-abiding owners
item.description: Colorado's Proposition KK, which imposes an additional 6.5% tax on gun purchases, was "intended to kill small gun stores," the owners of Hammer Down in Wheat Ridge said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1 dead after DHL cargo plane crashes in Lithuania; officials 'cannot rule out' terrorism
item.description: Lithuanian officials said they were investigating the cause of the crash, and "cannot rule out" terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Siarhei Satsiuk, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Macy's says employee hid up to $154 million in expenses
item.description: The company added that the person behind the conduct is no longer an employee and that the investigation didn't identify involvement by any other worker.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump Jr., Elon Musk joke about purchasing MSNBC
item.description: Musk’s exchange with Trump Jr. is similar to one he had with a Twitter user in 2017 after he was encouraged to purchase the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Baker Mayfield seeks $12M in lawsuit against father's company
item.description: An ongoing dispute between Tampa Bay Buccaneers quarterback Baker Mayfield and his family has boiled over.
item.dc:creator: Billy Gates, NewsNation

item.title: Airport workers strike in Charlotte
item.description: Employees at the Charlotte Douglas International Airport have gone on strike over wages during what’s expected to be the busiest travel week of the year with the Thanksgiving holiday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: New details emerge on BioLab fire that forced thousands to shelter outside Atlanta
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Federal authorities have released an update on the investigation into fires at the BioLab chemical plant near Atlanta that produced a toxic chemical cloud and forced nearby residents to shelter in place. The fires broke out Sept.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal judge dismisses cases of Jordanian men accused of attempted breach of Quantico gate in box truck
item.description: Two Jordanian men who were charged with unlawfully entering Marine Corps Base Quantico in Virginia in May have had their cases dismissed, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP senators squirm over sex-related allegations against Trump Cabinet picks
item.description: Republican senators are squirming over the rash of sexual misconduct allegations against President-elect Donald Trump's Cabinet picks, which they fear will become a focal point of Senate confirmation hearings next year.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge rejects 2nd Amendment argument from illegal immigrant living in Ohio charged over possession of 170 guns
item.description: A federal judge denied a request to dismiss a gun charge against an illegal immigrant in Ohio, rejecting the man's claim that he has Second Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chiefs' Patrick Mahomes fined over $14K for 'violent gesture' at Bills game
item.description: The fine comes after the star quarterback celebrated a touchdown pass with a hand gesture that appeared to imitate him holding a gun.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KRON4

item.title: Walmart executive says some prices might rise if Trump's tariffs take effect
item.description: A Walmart executive warned that President-elect Trump’s plans to raise tariffs on imports could drive the retail giant to increase the price of store items.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Earth bids farewell to its temporary 'mini moon': What it was, and where it's going
item.description: Currently, more than 2 million miles away, the object is too small and faint to see without a powerful telescope.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Storm inundates Northern California with rain, heavy snow. Thousands remain in the dark in Seattle
item.description: HEALDSBURG, Calif. (AP) — Heavy downpours fell over much of Northern California on Friday, causing small landslides, overflowing a river and flooding some streets, including in parts of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: NOAH BERGER and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump may move Space Command from Colorado to Alabama
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to move U.S. Space Command from Colorado Springs, Colorado to Huntsville, Alabama.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, ABC 4
